name: Comparison · Us vs them

do: 5-row checkmark / X grid (Us column vs Average Competitor column)
Never name competitors directly — say 'most {PRODUCT_CATEGORY}' or 'average {PRODUCT_CATEGORY}'
dont: no fake numbers (use ranges or 'varies' when honest)
no more than 5 rows (becomes a wall of text)

body: Amazon listing comparison image for {{PRODUCT_NAME}}, 2000×2000 square. Two-column comparison grid: left column labeled "{{BRAND_NAME}}" with {{BRAND_PRIMARY_COLOR_NAME}} background, right column labeled "Average {{PRODUCT_CATEGORY}}" with a neutral light-gray background. 5 rows. Each row shows a feature claim with a green checkmark in the brand column and a red X or category-range value in the competitor column. The 5 features are derived from: {{FEATURE_LIST}}. Headline above the grid in {{BRAND_FONT_PRIMARY}}: a single defensible differentiator (≤ 8 words) in the {{BRAND_VOICE_ADJECTIVES}} voice. No named competitor brand.

angles.name: 5-row checkmark grid (default)
angles.description: Classic us-vs-them grid with checkmarks and X marks.

angles.name: Stacked bar visual
angles.description: Each feature is a horizontal bar showing brand vs category average proportionally.
angles.prompt_modifier: Replace the checkmark grid with 4 stacked horizontal bars. Each bar shows the brand value (filled in {{BRAND_ACCENT_COLOR}}) against the category average (filled in neutral gray). Numeric value labels at the right of each bar.
